# irtsim

[![CRAN
status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/irtsim)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=irtsim)
[![CRAN
downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/irtsim)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=irtsim)
[![License: GPL
v3](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPL_v3-blue.svg)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0)

Monte Carlo simulation-based sample-size planning for item response
theory.

## Installation

``` r

install.packages("irtsim")
```

## Quick start

``` r

library(irtsim)

design  <- irt_design(
  model = "1PL", n_items = 20,
  item_params = list(b = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 20))
)
study   <- irt_study(design, sample_sizes = c(200, 400, 800))
results <- irt_simulate(study, iterations = 100, seed = 42)

summary(results)
plot(results)
recommended_n(summary(results), criterion = "rmse", threshold = 0.20)
```

## What it supports

- **Models:** 1PL, 2PL, graded response model (GRM)
- **Missingness:** MCAR, MAR, booklet designs, linking designs
- **Criteria:** MSE, bias, RMSE, SE, coverage, Monte Carlo SE, plus
  user-defined via `criterion_fn`
- **Misspecification:** generate with one model, fit with another
- **Parallelization:** `irt_simulate(parallel = TRUE)` via
  `future.apply`

## Reference

Schroeders, U., & Gnambs, T. (2025). Sample size planning in item
response theory: A 10-decision framework. *Advances in Methods and
Practices in Psychological Science.*
<https://doi.org/10.1177/25152459251314798>

## License

GPL (\>= 3)

# Package index

## Core Pipeline

The three-step workflow: specify a design, add study conditions, and run
the simulation.

- [`irt_design()`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/irt_design.md)
  : Create an IRT Design Specification
- [`irt_study()`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/irt_study.md)
  : Define Study Conditions for an IRT Simulation
- [`irt_simulate()`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/irt_simulate.md)
  : Run an IRT Monte Carlo Simulation

## Results Analysis

Summarize, visualize, and extract recommendations from simulation
results.

- [`summary(`*`<irt_results>`*`)`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/summary.irt_results.md)
  : Summarize IRT Simulation Results
- [`plot(`*`<irt_results>`*`)`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/plot.irt_results.md)
  : Plot IRT Simulation Results
- [`plot(`*`<summary_irt_results>`*`)`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/plot.summary_irt_results.md)
  : Plot Summary of IRT Simulation Results
- [`recommended_n()`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/recommended_n.md)
  : Find the Minimum Sample Size Meeting a Criterion Threshold

## Utilities

Helper functions for parameter generation and planning.

- [`irt_params_2pl()`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/irt_params_2pl.md)
  : Generate 2PL Item Parameters
- [`irt_params_grm()`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/irt_params_grm.md)
  : Generate GRM Item Parameters
- [`irt_iterations()`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/irt_iterations.md)
  : Compute Required Monte Carlo Replications

## Print Methods

- [`print(`*`<irt_design>`*`)`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/print.irt_design.md)
  : Print an IRT Design
- [`print(`*`<irt_study>`*`)`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/print.irt_study.md)
  : Print an IRT Study
- [`print(`*`<irt_results>`*`)`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/print.irt_results.md)
  : Print an IRT Simulation Result
- [`print(`*`<summary_irt_results>`*`)`](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/reference/print.summary_irt_results.md)
  : Print Summary of IRT Simulation Results

# Articles

### Get Started

- [Planning a Sample Size for an IRT
  Study](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/articles/irtsim.md):
- [Choosing Item Parameters for Sample-Size
  Planning](https://sward1.github.io/irtsim/articles/choosing-item-parameters.md):
